items.prompt: Should people be free to choose diets that are obviously unhealthy, even when doctors advise against them?

ChatGPT.reasoning: People should have the autonomy to choose their diets, but education and medical advice are crucial to help them understand the risks of unhealthy choices. Ultimately, freedom of choice is important unless it causes harm to others.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: People should have the autonomy to choose their diets, but education and medical advice are crucial to help them understand the risks of unhealthy choices. Ultimately, freedom of choice is important unless it causes...

Claude.reasoning: Yes, people should retain autonomy over their dietary choices. While medical advice should be respected and accessible, individual liberty to make informed decisions—even poor ones—is fundamental to personal freedom and dignity.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Yes, people should retain autonomy over their dietary choices. While medical advice should be respected and accessible, individual liberty to make informed decisions—even poor ones—is fundamental to personal freedom and...

Gemini.reasoning: Yes, individuals should retain autonomy over their dietary choices, even if those choices are deemed unhealthy by medical professionals, as long as they understand the risks. Personal freedom includes the right to make choices about one's own body and lifestyle.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: Yes, individuals should retain autonomy over their dietary choices, even if those choices are deemed unhealthy by medical professionals, as long as they understand the risks. Personal freedom includes the right to make...

Grok.reasoning: Individuals should have autonomy over their own bodies and diets, even if unhealthy, as paternalistic intervention undermines personal freedom without consent.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Individuals should have autonomy over their own bodies and diets, even if unhealthy, as paternalistic intervention undermines personal freedom without consent.

items.prompt: Should people be free to choose diets that are obviously unhealthy, even when doctors advise against them?

ChatGPT.reasoning: People have the right to make personal choices, including diets, even if unhealthy, as long as they do not harm others; medical advice is guidance, not a mandate.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: People have the right to make personal choices, including diets, even if unhealthy, as long as they do not harm others; medical advice is guidance, not a mandate.

Claude.reasoning: Yes—autonomy matters, and people learn better through consequences and education than coercion; the real work is making healthy choices accessible and affordable, not removing freedom to choose poorly.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Yes—autonomy matters, and people learn better through consequences and education than coercion; the real work is making healthy choices accessible and affordable, not removing freedom to choose poorly.

Gemini.reasoning: To deny individuals the freedom to choose their own diet, however ill-advised, assumes the state or medical professionals have a superior right to bodily autonomy. This paternalistic view undermines fundamental personal liberty.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: To deny individuals the freedom to choose their own diet, however ill-advised, assumes the state or medical professionals have a superior right to bodily autonomy. This paternalistic view undermines fundamental personal...

Grok.reasoning: Forcing diets harms autonomy and society via resentment/noncompliance (billions affected), while free choice promotes responsibility; individual health harms are outweighed.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Forcing diets harms autonomy and society via resentment/noncompliance (billions affected), while free choice promotes responsibility; individual health harms are outweighed.
